title: [security] C OSCORE compute_nonce violates RFC 8613 layout (S at byte 6, Sender ID overlaps PIV)
description: lichen/subsys/lichen/oscore/oscore.c compute_nonce() (lines 1165-1205) builds the AES-CCM nonce incorrectly for any non-empty sender ID: (1) OSCORE_NONCE_S_POS=6 (oscore/include/lichen/oscore.h:105) writes the ID-length byte at nonce byte 6; RFC 8613 Section 5.2 places S at byte 0. (2) Sender ID is right-aligned ending at byte 12 (start = OSCORE_NONCE_LEN - sender_id_len, oscore.c:1187) so the ID field overlaps the PIV field (PIV also ends at byte 12) - ID bytes XOR on top of PIV bytes. The header comment (oscore.c:529-535) documents the misreading ('7th byte overlaps'). Empirically verified against RFC 8613 test vectors: C.4 (empty sender ID) accidentally MATCHES (bug masked when id_len==0); C.5 (sender_id=0x00): C-construction nonce be35ae297d2dADe9... vs RFC bf35ae297d2dACe9... - ciphertext DIFFERS; C.8 (own-PIV response, ID 0x01): ID XORs into byte 12 on top of PIV - DIFFERS. Consequence: the Zephyr node cannot interop with Python (aiocoap, verified correct), Rust (once 2auf.48.3 fixed), or any RFC-conformant peer for any context with non-empty sender/recipient IDs - i.e. all real EDHOC-derived contexts. Fix: S at byte 0, ID left-padded into bytes 1..6, PIV left-padded into bytes 7..12, then XOR common_iv. Land RFC C.4-C.8 vectors as C tests in the same change (no C test currently calls oscore_protect_request at all). Distinct from 2auf.48.3 which is Rust-scoped.
